methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Precision AI is a Series A–stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector founded by Daniel McCann, having raised $20M to date. The relevant comp universe consists of 8 private companies operating in drones and autonomous systems, including Zipline, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air. No public comparables exist in this narrowly defined space.

Only one valuation method proved applicable given data constraints. The Stage-Progression approach uses total capital raised ($20M) as a proxy for post-money valuation and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression, yielding an implied valuation of $40M. A peer-median analysis was not feasible; while 5 same-sector peers exist, insufficient datapoints met the filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier peers (fewer than 3 in each category), making a reliable multiple-based cross-check unavailable without introducing distortion from broader sector medians.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $34M (low) to $46M (high), with a median of $40M. This range carries material uncertainty: the absence of comparable transaction or valuation data at identical stage and scale limits conviction in the step-up multiple applied. The valuation is most sensitive to whether Precision AI's capital efficiency and growth trajectory align with the assumed 2.0x multiplier or warrant acceleration or moderation thereof.
